base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.general_idea: Remove the abstract modifier from the BehaviorBase class to match the non-abstract nature of the original JavaFX internal API.
base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.why_exists: The original JavaFX BehaviorBase is not abstract; keeping it abstract would prevent direct instantiation or subclassing patterns that rely on the concrete base class, causing logic errors or preventing usage in scenarios where the base class is instantiated directly.
base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.before: public abstract class BehaviorBase<C extends Control, S extends SkinBase<C>> {
base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.after: public class BehaviorBase<C extends Control, S extends SkinBase<C>> {

base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.general_idea: Replace TreeItem with custom BreadCrumbItem model to support first/last state tracking and decouple from JavaFX TreeItem.
base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.why_exists: The code relies on BreadCrumbItem methods like isFirst(), isLast(), and setFirst/setLast which do not exist on TreeItem, causing compile errors.

base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.general_idea: Replace Button with ButtonBase for crumb nodes to allow more flexible control types.
base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.why_exists: The factory now returns ButtonBase, and the code assigns it to ButtonBase variables; using Button would cause type mismatch if non-Button controls are used.

base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.general_idea: Switch from style classes to PseudoClasses for first/last state styling.
base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.why_exists: The new logic uses pseudoClassStateChanged(FIRST/LAST) instead of adding/removing style classes, requiring the PseudoClass constants.

base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.general_idea: Update event handler registration to use BreadCrumbItem's childrenModificationEvent.
base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.why_exists: TreeItem.childrenModificationEvent() is no longer used; BreadCrumbItem provides its own event type.

base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.general_idea: Reorganize imports and add missing ones.
base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.why_exists: Required for new types like BreadCrumbItem and PseudoClass.

base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.general_idea: Replace deprecated or removed Styles constant with explicit CSS style class string.
base/src/main/java/atlantafx/base/contro.why_exists: The Styles class or specific constant may have been removed or refactored in the newer JavaFX version, causing a compile error if the import and usage are not updated.
